summary: Navigation hierarchy atom with optional home entry, current-page metadata, hidden separators, and shared nav/list semantics across adapters.
philosophy: Keep Breadcrumb focused on representing page hierarchy while routing and link resolution stay product-owned.
Keep the current item explicit through aria-current so visual state and screen-reader state remain aligned.
Use the synced Figma breadcrumb component and item/separator parts as the compact visual baseline.

useWhen: A page needs to show its location inside a broader hierarchy.
The current page should be announced as the current breadcrumb item.
React, Vue, and Svelte consumers need the same nav/list/separator semantics.
avoidWhen: The UI needs primary site navigation rather than location context.
The hierarchy cannot identify a truthful current page.
Routing behavior, link resolution, or page ownership is still unclear.

automationCovers: Core and adapter tests for nav/list structure, home entry, current item metadata, and separator hiding.
Storybook introduction and taxonomy tests in React, Vue, and Svelte apps.
manualReviewRequired: Visual review against the synced light and dark Figma breadcrumb frames.
Product-level review that the current item, page title, and URL hierarchy match.
notes: Breadcrumb is covered by the canonical family semantic registry with data-component=breadcrumb.

openQuestions: Should a future managed breadcrumb helper infer the current item from router state, or should routing remain fully product-owned?
